title: Financial Accountant - AU Company
description:
Who is Bare?

Bare is the new way to funeral. We are a holistic end of life care provider, allowing our families to grieve in their own way, at their own pace, knowing their loved one is cared for by a team who truly understand their needs.



By combining empathy with technology, we’re creating end-of-life experiences that are simple, personalized and affordable. In only five years, we’ve served more than 30,000 families, have become Australia’s most awarded end-of-life provider (with awards in customer service, innovation and digital disruption), and have built a team of more than 100 people committed to changing the way Australians deal with end-of-life. And we’re just getting started.



We’re looking for a passionate and detail-oriented Financial Accountant to join our high-performing remote team. If you're a proactive finance professional who thrives on ownership, continuous improvement, and meaningful work, we’d love to meet you.



What You’ll Be Doing

As our Financial Accountant, you’ll be a key player in ensuring accurate, insightful, and timely financial reporting. You’ll collaborate across teams, drive process improvement, and uphold the financial integrity of our mission.



Key Responsibilities:

-Own the Month-End Close

Lead all aspects of EOM: journal entries, reconciliations, accruals, and financial statement prep.

-Deliver Insightful Reporting

Produce monthly and quarterly management reports, including variance and trend analysis.

-Ensure Regulatory Compliance

Prepare BAS, IAS, and statutory filings, ensuring alignment with AASB and tax standards.

-Champion Continuous Improvement

Streamline workflows, introduce automation, and enhance internal controls.

-Collaborate Cross-Functionally

Partner with Marketing, Operations, and other non-finance teams to align on budget and strategy.

-Support Strategic Projects

Get involved in cost analysis, ERP upgrades, and special projects that shape our financial future.



Why Join Bare?

Make a Meaningful Impact: Your work directly supports families during one of the most vulnerable times of their lives.

Remote-First Flexibility: Work from anywhere in Australia with the tools and autonomy to succeed.

Mission-Driven Culture: Join a team that leads with empathy and integrity—where your voice matters.

Growth & Innovation: Help shape scalable systems in a business that's growing fast and disrupting the norm.



About You

You’re not just technically sharp, you bring heart and high standards to everything you do. You thrive in fast-paced environments and care about doing work that matters.



Must-Haves:

-Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or similar.

-CPA/CA qualification (or in-progress).

-2–3+ years' experience in financial accounting, ideally in service-led sectors.

-Familiarity with Australian accounting standards, BAS/IAS, and financial reporting.

-Understanding of various accounting standards relevant to Bare and consolidations of many entities.

-Attention to detail and excellent reconciliation skills

-Strong ERP (MYOB, Xero, SAP) and Excel skills.

-Strong Excel/Sheets skills and automation of manual processes in your mindset-DNA



Nice-to-Haves:

• Experience in mission-driven sectors (healthcare, aged care, funeral services).

• Adaptability in high-growth or high-volume environments.

• Resilient, working with little or no supervision.

• Experience of managing international teams, interaction with multi-entity organizations, and experience within companies with a minimum annual revenue of $50 million.

• Handle ambiguity, competing priorities, and strict deadlines. Create and implement scalable processes, including training. Integrate AI and automation.



Core Values We Look For

• Empathy: You care deeply about people—colleagues and customers alike.

• Accountability: You take ownership and deliver results with pride.

• Clarity: You communicate financial information in a clear, human way.

• Curiosity: You’re always looking for a better way and love solving problems.



Perks & Benefits

-Fully remote work setup with flexible hours

-Purpose-driven work with real human impact

-Growth and learning opportunities in a scale-up environment

-Supportive, inclusive team culture



Please include your resume when submitting your application. Thank you!

title: Solar Sales Support and Solar Finance Manager
description:
This position serves as the operational backbone of the sales organization, coordinating between sales representatives, finance partners, project management, and accounting to ensure projects move efficiently from contract to installation and funding.



Key Responsibilities



Sales Intake & Deal Review

    •    Review and process incoming residential solar contracts for completeness and accuracy

    •    Verify customer documentation including contracts, utility bills, site data, and financing information

    •    Ensure deals meet company guidelines before submission to operations and engineering

    •    Maintain accurate CRM records for all incoming projects



Finance Coordination

    •    Submit and manage financing applications with lenders i.e. Lightreach, GoodLeap etc.

    •    Work directly with financing partners to resolve stipulations or approval conditions

    •    Assist sales reps in structuring deals that meet lender guidelines

    •    Monitor approval timelines and communicate status updates to sales leadership



Project Financial Accuracy (True-Up)

    •    Perform project financial true-ups after engineering and installation

    •    Confirm system size, equipment changes, and cost adjustments

    •    Ensure project economics align with approved financing and sales agreements

    •    Work with internal teams to correct discrepancies prior to installation or funding



Final Project Funding

    •    Compile and submit final documentation packages required for lender funding

    •    Coordinate with installation and operations teams to confirm completion milestones

    •    Track funding timelines and ensure projects are funded promptly

    •    Resolve lender or documentation issues that delay payment



Pipeline & Reporting

    •    Monitor the full sales pipeline from contract to funding

    •    Track project status and identify bottlenecks in the process

    •    Provide weekly reports on approvals, installs, and funded projects

    •    Assist leadership with forecasting funded revenue



Qualifications

    •    3+ years experience in residential solar operations, sales support, or project coordination

    •    Strong understanding of solar financing structures (loan, PPA, lease)

    •    Experience working with solar lenders and funding processes

    •    Proficiency with CRM systems and project management software

    •    Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills

    •    Ability to coordinate across sales, operations, and finance teams



Preferred Experience

    •    Experience working with residential solar EPC companies

    •    Knowledge of interconnection, permitting, and installation workflows

    •    Experience managing large volumes of sales submissions



Key Traits for Success

    •    Highly organized and process driven

    •    Strong communication skills with both sales teams and lenders

    •    Ability to identify and solve deal issues before they delay projects

    •    Comfortable working in a fast paced sales environment

title: Photoshop Expert Needed for Realistic T-Shirt Mockups (Design Placement Specialist)
description:
Job Description



We are looking for a Photoshop expert to help create realistic ad creatives for a clothing brand.



The main task is placing t-shirt or hoodie designs onto lifestyle photos and making them look completely natural and realistic.



This role requires strong Photoshop compositing skills. This is NOT basic Photoshop work. If you are very comfortable working with clothing mockups and design placement, this may be a great fit.



This will start as a part-time role with approximately 10–30 creatives per week, with the possibility of more work in the future as we expand to additional stores.



Payment will be task-based, but the expected earnings should be roughly equivalent to $6–$8 per hour depending on speed and experience.



Main Responsibilities



You will receive:



• a lifestyle inspiration image

• a t-shirt design file

• simple instructions



Your job will be to:



• remove the existing design on the shirt

• place our design realistically on the clothing

• match lighting, shadows, and fabric texture

• make the final image look natural and ready for advertising



The final result should look like the design was actually printed on the shirt in the photo.



Required Photoshop Skills



Strong experience with realistic design placement on clothing is required.



You should be comfortable using techniques such as:



• Perspective Warp

• Displacement Maps

• Blend Modes

• Shadow and Lighting adjustments

• Wrinkle and fabric distortion

• Noise / grain matching

• Color grading and color matching



If you have experience creating realistic clothing mockups, that is a big plus.



Small AI Tools in the Workflow



At the beginning of the process, you may sometimes use simple AI tools to help prepare the base image.



Example tasks:



• removing the original design from a shirt

• changing the person wearing the shirt

• slightly modifying the environment/background



These tasks are very simple and easy, and we can teach you how to do them.



However, these AI tools are ONLY used to prepare the image.



???? The final mockup must be created in Photoshop.



Using AI to generate the final result is completely unacceptable. If you plan to generate the final image using AI instead of properly placing the design in Photoshop, please do not apply.



Important



This job is specifically for someone who is skilled at:



realistic t-shirt design placement and clothing mockups in Photoshop.



If you have done similar work before, please include examples in your application.



Test Task



Before hiring, we will provide one small test task.



You will receive:



• one inspiration image

• one design file



Your task will be to place the design onto the shirt realistically.



This helps us evaluate your Photoshop skills before moving forward.



Future Opportunities



We currently need 10–25 creatives per week, but we run multiple online stores.



If the collaboration goes well, there will likely be more work and long-term opportunities in the future.

title: Virtual Assistant – Short-Term Rental Operations (Airbnb/VRBO)
description:
???? About Damian's Hosting Hub



We are a growing vacation rental management company based in Modesto, California. We manage Airbnb and short-term rental properties for property owners — handling everything from guest communication to cleaning coordination to listing optimization.



We're looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to help run our day-to-day short-term rental operations. This is NOT a generic VA role — you will learn the vacation rental business inside and out.





???? What You'll Be Doing



GUEST MESSAGING & COMMUNICATION

• Respond to guest inquiries and booking requests quickly (target: under 15 minutes)

• Send pre-arrival messages — check-in instructions, house rules, local recommendations

• Handle mid-stay guest questions, concerns, and requests

• Manage checkout messaging and request guest reviews

• Communicate across Airbnb, VRBO, and direct booking channels

• De-escalate guest issues professionally and know when to escalate to management



LISTING CREATION & OPTIMIZATION

• Create new property listings on Airbnb, VRBO, and other booking platforms

• Write compelling, SEO-optimized listing titles and descriptions

• Upload and organize property photos

• Set and adjust pricing, minimum stays, and availability

• Monitor and respond to guest reviews

• Keep listing details, amenities, and house rules accurate and updated



SERVICE COORDINATION & OPERATIONS

• Schedule and coordinate cleaning crews between guest turnovers

• Coordinate maintenance and repair requests with local vendors

• Track supply inventory and flag when restocking is needed

• Manage the turnover calendar — ensure no gaps between checkout, cleaning, and check-in

• Coordinate with property owners on access, maintenance, and updates



ADMIN & REPORTING

• Maintain organized records of bookings, revenue, and expenses

• Update CRM and property management tools

• Monitor booking calendars across platforms to prevent double-bookings

• Assist with onboarding new properties (photos checklist, amenity inventory, listing setup)

• Generate basic performance reports (occupancy rates, revenue, guest ratings)

• Help manage social media posting and content scheduling





? Requirements



• Previous experience as a VA in short-term rentals, property management, or hospitality (strongly preferred)

• Familiarity with Airbnb, VRBO, or similar booking platforms

• Excellent written English — guest-facing messages must be professional, warm, and error-free

• Strong organizational skills — you'll juggle multiple properties, guests, and service providers at the same time

• Reliable high-speed internet connection and a quiet workspace

• Available to work flexible hours including some weekend coverage for guest messaging

• Proficient with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Calendar)

• Tech-savvy — comfortable learning new software and tools quickly

• Self-starter who works independently without needing constant supervision

• Must have your own computer with a working webcam and microphone





? Bonus Skills (Not Required But a Big Plus)



• Experience with property management software (Hostaway, Guesty, Hospitable)

• Experience with GoHighLevel CRM

• Familiarity with dynamic pricing tools (PriceLabs, Beyond Pricing, Wheelhouse)

• Canva or basic graphic design skills

• Bilingual (English/Spanish)

• Social media management experience





???? Compensation & Benefits



• $100/week — paid weekly, consistently every week

• 100% remote — work from home

• Stable, long-term position with a growing company

• Direct access to the owner — no corporate layers or red tape

• Opportunity to grow into a senior role as we add more properties

• Full training provided on our specific systems and processes





???? How to Apply



Please include in your application:



1. A brief summary of your relevant experience (especially any short-term rental or hospitality experience)

2. A short paragraph explaining why you'd be a great fit for this role

3. Your availability — what hours can you work? What is your time zone?

4. Links to any Airbnb or VRBO listings you've managed (if applicable)

5. Do you have experience with any property management software? If so, which ones?



We respond to every qualified applicant. If you're the right fit, we'll schedule a quick video interview to get to know you.



We look forward to hearing from you!

title: HR Admin Support
description:
Role Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented HR and Admin Assistant to be the main point of contact for all staff inquiries. Key responsibilities include managing daily operations, supporting recruitment and staffing efforts, and ensuring the accuracy of staff scheduling. This is a part-time role, requiring 4–5 hours each business day.



Key Responsibilities

- Recruitment: Assist with administrative duties relevant to recruitment and onboarding/offboarding

- Rostering: Manage daily shift approvals, adjustments, and attendance tracking using Found-u and RosterLab.

- Staff Support: Act as the first point of contact for staff inquiries regarding schedules and basic HR policies.

- General Admin: Maintain accurate records, manage
 correspondence, and perform data entry as needed.

- Compliance: Ensure staff documentation (IDs, certifications) is up to date.



Requirements

- Experience: 2+ years experience in similar position

- Software: Hands-on experience with found-u or Rosterlab highly desirable

- HubSpot: Previous experience using HubSpot or similar CRM is highly desirable.

- Communication: High-level English proficiency (written and verbal).

- Availability: Must be able to check in/work across all 5 business days.



Application Process

To apply for this position, please create and submit a two-minute video addressing the following five points:

- Introduce yourself and detail your relevant experience in rostering and Human Resources (HR).

- Highlight your key professional strengths.

- Describe a typical workday in your most recent role.

- Specify your current availability, expressed in Melbourne time (AEST).

- Detail the configuration of your home office setup.

title: Social Media Manager (Remote – Ecommerce Brands)
description:
We are looking for a creative and thoughtful Social Media Manager to help manage the social media for two ecommerce brands. You will help plan, create, and manage content for Instagram +
. This is a remote part-time role, around 3–4 days of work per week (schedule to be discussed). This role is not just about posting content. We want someone who understands good storytelling, strong hooks, and engaging posts.



About the Brands:

Oono - Oono creates premium backpacks designed for modern professionals and frequent travelers.

Our brand focuses on organization, reliability, and simple smart design.



Mylk Fat - Mylk Fat is a natural skincare brand built around grass-fed beef tallow.

The brand focuses on simple ingredients, skin nourishment, and premium skincare.



What We’re Looking For:

We want someone who can create thoughtful social media content, not generic posts. We want to see:



• More Reels and carousels

• Strong hooks and text overlays

• Storytelling and varied post formats

• Posts that feel clean, thoughtful, and premium



You should take the time to understand the brand before creating content. Lazy or generic AI content is not what we’re looking for. AI can be used as a tool, but the final content should feel intentional and on-brand.



Responsibilities:

• Plan and schedule social media posts

• Create Reels, carousels, and posts

• Write engaging captions and hooks

• Suggest content ideas and formats

• Monitor performance and improve content

• Reply to comments and messages when needed



Replies should feel natural and thoughtful, not robotic. Stay up to date with social media trends while keeping the brand quality high.



Requirements:

• Experience managing social media for brands

• Strong understanding of Instagram content (Reels, carousels, hooks)

• Good writing skills

• Good visual taste for premium brands

• Able to work independently

• Able to receive feedback and improve work



We prefer fewer high-quality posts rather than many low-quality posts.



How to Apply:

Please send:

• Your resume

• Links to social media accounts you have managed

• Examples of posts, Reels, or carousels you created



To confirm you read the job post, please start your application with the word:

Thoughtful



Applications without examples of previous work will not be considered.

title: Marketing Manager/Analyst
description:
We are looking for a sharp, analytical, and creatively-minded Quota & Content Performance Manager to take full ownership of our weekly content quota process. This is a high-trust, high-leverage role that sits at the heart of our agency's performance engine.

You will be responsible for building weekly content plans ("quotas") for each creator on our roster, analysing Instagram performance data to determine what is working and what isn't, and producing clear, structured performance reports that our management team uses to have meaningful conversations with creators.

This is not a posting or scheduling role. This is a strategic content operations role — you need to be able to think analytically about performance data AND have the creative intuition to understand why certain content formats resonate with different types of audiences.

title: Customer Service Representative
description:
IMPORTANT: Replies are not monitored here. To apply, please complete this form: 



Rove Miles is building the world’s first universal airline mile. Through our platform, travelers can earn miles on everyday spend and travel bookings to work towards a free annual vacation. We partner with major loyalty programs, airlines, hotels, and merchants to make premium travel more accessible to everyone.



Since our launch two months ago, we’ve been featured in The Wall Street Journal, Forbes, and TechCrunch and have already gained tens of thousands of users.



What You’ll Do:

- Handle customer support inquiries related to flight and hotel bookings made through our platform (including changes, cancellations) + Rove Shopping purchases—ensuring proper miles posting and troubleshooting missing rewards.

- Liaise directly with airlines, loyalty programs, hotel partners, and suppliers (including GDS or aggregator systems) to resolve booking issues.

- Manage escalations involving complex itineraries, miles redemption questions, and travel disruptions.

- Educate customers on how to maximize the value of Rove Miles.

- Maintain accurate case logs in our CRM and proactively follow up to ensure customer satisfaction.

- Collaborate with our product and operations teams by surfacing recurring issues and ideas for improving the user experience.



What We’re Looking For:

- 3+ years of customer support experience at an online travel agency (OTA) or travel company.

(e.g., Expedia, 
, Hopper, Priceline, or direct airline/hotel desk experience).

- Solid understanding of flight and hotel booking workflows—PNRs, fare rules, and common post-booking issues.

- Experience handling multi-channel support 
, chat, phone).

- Empathetic and calm under pressure, especially when dealing with disrupted travel plans.

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

- Comfortable with technology—can pick up new platforms quickly.



PLEASE ONLY APPLY IF YOU HAVE AT LEAST 3 YEARS OF EXPERIENCE DOING CUSTOMER SERVICE FOR AN ONLINE TRAVEL AGENCY.

title: Part-Time Meta Ads Specialist (Lead Generation Focus) – Remote
description:
PART-TIME META ADS SPECIALIST (LEAD GENERATION FOCUS) – REMOTE



We are a growing digital marketing agency looking for an experienced Meta Ads specialist to help manage and optimize several lead-generation advertising accounts.



This role focuses on performance marketing and lead generation, not social media posting or brand awareness campaigns.



Some of the accounts you will work on manage $10,000 – $60,000 per month in Meta ad spend, so experience managing performance campaigns at scale is important.



We are looking for someone who enjoys improving lead generation performance through creative testing, messaging experiments, and campaign optimization.



You will work on a mix of larger national lead generation accounts and smaller local business campaigns.



This is a long-term part-time role for someone who enjoys optimizing campaigns and testing new creative ideas.



TYPES OF ACCOUNTS



You will help manage campaigns for:



• Large lead-generation advertising accounts with monthly ad spend ranging from $10,000 – $60,000

• Local service businesses generating inbound leads

• Local retail businesses focused on driving store visits and inquiries



Campaign objectives may include:



• Lead generation using Meta instant forms

• Local lead campaigns

• Store visit / get directions campaigns

• Testing and optimizing new ad creatives



RESPONSIBILITIES



• Build, manage, and optimize Meta advertising campaigns

• Manage lead generation campaigns using instant forms

• Monitor campaign performance and optimize budgets, targeting, and creatives

• Assist with creative testing (images, short videos, ad copy)

• Ensure leads are properly captured and delivered to our systems

• Provide simple performance reporting



A key part of this role is testing new ad creatives and messaging angles regularly to improve campaign performance.



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE



• Strong experience using Meta Ads Manager

• Experience running lead generation campaigns

• Experience with Meta instant forms

• Experience working with Meta Pixel and Conversion API (CAPI)

• Understanding of CRM integrations or lead delivery workflows

• Ability to analyze campaign performance metrics (CTR, CPL, CPA, etc.)



COMMUNICATION SKILLS



• Strong written and spoken English required

• Must be comfortable communicating with a remote team

• Ability to clearly explain campaign performance and ideas



CREATIVE SKILLS (IMPORTANT)



You do not need to be a professional designer, but you should be comfortable creating or editing simple ad creatives.



Examples include:



• Image ads with text overlays

• Short video ads (12–30 seconds)

• Basic edits using tools such as Canva, CapCut, or similar



NICE TO HAVE



• Experience with Google Ads

• Experience working with agencies

• Experience with local lead generation campaigns

• Experience with Messenger or conversation-based campaigns



HOURS AND COMPENSATION



• Part-time role (approximately 15–20 hours per week)

• Compensation: $1,500 – $2,000 per month depending on experience and skill level

• Fully remote



TO APPLY



Please answer the following questions when applying:



1. What is the largest monthly Meta Ads budget you have personally managed?



2. Describe a lead generation campaign you successfully managed.



3. Do you have experience running Meta lead ads with instant forms?



4. What tools do you use to create ad creatives (images or short videos)?



5. Do you have experience setting up Meta Pixel or Conversion API (CAPI)?



6. In your opinion, what percentage of Meta campaign performance typically comes from creative vs targeting, and why?



To confirm you read this entire job post, please include the word LEADS at the beginning of your application and tell Mary the largest Meta Ads monthly budget you have personally managed.

title: Google Ads/ PPC Manager
description:
**PLEASE ATTACH YOUR RESUME LINK TO YOUR APPLICATION**



The Google Ads / PPC Manager will own the planning, execution, and optimization of paid search campaigns across Google Ads and other digital platforms. This role is responsible for driving measurable revenue growth, maintaining accurate attribution and tracking, and providing structured reporting and insights to inform business decisions. The ideal candidate combines hands-on campaign management with strong analytical skills and a strategic mindset to ensure campaigns meet both efficiency and profitability goals.

 

Responsibilities

Manage day-to-day Google Ads campaigns, including Search, Shopping, and Performance Max campaigns.

Monitor retail media and paid search performance (ACoS, ROAS, CPC, conversions) and make data-driven recommendations.

Implement search terms, targeting, bidding, and budget strategies to maximize ROI.

Maintain tracking integrity and ensure accurate attribution across campaigns.

Analyze campaign performance, generate actionable insights, and produce structured reporting using GA4, dashboards, and spreadsheets.

Own asset management and optimization strategy for full-funnel campaigns.

Execute retargeting and remarketing strategies to drive conversions.

Collaborate cross-functionally with SEO, CRO, Web Development, Paid Media, and Merchandising teams to ensure campaigns are aligned with broader business priorities.

Support additional ad types such as native ads as needed.

Maintain detailed documentation, logs, and performance tracking to support testing and decision-making.

Competencies and Qualifications



Must-haves

5+ years of experience in Google Ads performance analysis and campaign management

Strong bid, budget, and ROI management skills (ACoS, ROAS, CPC, conversions)

Expertise in retail profitability optimization

Strong search term and targeting strategy skills

Data analysis and reporting proficiency (GA4, dashboards, spreadsheets)

Experience with attribution and tracking integrity

Hands-on experience with asset management and full-funnel optimization strategies

Knowledge of the Shopify ecosystem

Ability to execute retargeting and remarketing campaigns

Experience managing other types of ads (native ads)



Nice-to-haves

Knowledge of Meta Ads

Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or related field

Experience with creative testing and messaging optimization



Key Qualities

Analytical – Able to interpret data, identify trends, and translate insights into actionable improvements for campaigns.

Goal-Oriented – Focused on driving measurable results, meeting KPIs, and optimizing performance across all paid media efforts.

Big Picture Thinker – Understands how paid search campaigns fit into broader e-commerce strategy and overall business objectives.







What We Offer



100% Remote Work – Work from anywhere in the Philippines.

Career Growth Opportunities – Gain hands-on experience, and continuous learning.

Collaborative & Innovative Team – Work alongside skilled professionals who value excellence.

title: Video Editor for Social Ads — $40 Per Video
description:
We're FULCIO, a 3+ year old marketing agency in Europe. Video is the core of everything we do, and we're looking for one more editor to join the team.



The work: You'll edit short-form video ads (around 30 seconds - 1 minute each) for Instagram and
. These aren't template videos, they're story-driven ads designed to stop the scroll and sell. You'll get raw A-roll, B-roll, and creative direction, then bring the story to life through pacing, music, transitions and hooks.



The pay: $40 per completed video. Edit 8 per week and that's $1,280/week, paid consistently by a company that's been around for 3+ years and isn't going anywhere.



What we need from you:

- Minimum 3 years editing in Adobe Premiere Pro

-A portfolio with e-commerce or direct-response ad work

-The ability to create hooks that make people stop scrolling

-Willingness to learn, take feedback, and grow with the team



What you get:

-Creative freedom on every edit

-Daily feedback so you improve fast

-A real team (not a solo gig)

description:
THE COMPANY

We specialize in the creation of captivating and immersive content for corporate clients, offering comprehensive content design, production, and creative services. Working with Avalon Creative Group presents an exciting opportunity for individuals seeking to collaborate with renowned brands, while also offering a continuous learning environment that fosters personal and professional growth.



JOB DESCRIPTION

Avalon Creative Group is seeking a talented and experienced video editor and 3D animator based in the Philippines to work with us as an independent contractor. Our work includes videos to inspire audiences at corporate events, on-demand marketing webinars, internal corporate training and sales enablement, product demos, highlight reels, social media ads, interviews, customer testimonials and more.



Our goal is to establish a long term working relationship with a creative, reliable and ambitious video professional. After successfully completing an initial trial period, the right person will become an integral member of our creative team on a full-time contract basis.



RESPONSIBILITIES

> Edit high quality videos from a variety of sources, including single-camera, multi-camera, stock footage, AI generated video, graphic elements, music, sound effects, etc.

> Effectively and efficiently generate video footage and elements using the latest AI tools

> Manage accuracy and correction of color and audio

> Understand and apply corporate branding and style guidelines and templates (MOGRTs)

> Manage, organize and backup video assets and deliverables, including raw footage, graphics, sound files, stock footage, projects, files, etc.

> Adhere to budgeted time constraints while incorporating client feedback and changes through multiple rounds of review and revisions

> Contribute to and consistently apply efficient workflow processes and standards (SOPs)

> Document guidelines and process details for tea
mbers and clients

> Pursue ongoing professional development and skill enhancements



REQUIRED SKILLS

> Having a proven track record of punctuality, reliability and professionalism is essential

> Excellent attention to detail and adeptness at managing multiple concurrent projects is required

> Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively in a remote team environment

> English fluency (written and spoken) along with excellent communication skills are essential

> You must be based in the Philippines with fast, reliable internet access and a robust computer

> You must have a quiet workspace where you can work without distractions and join Zoom calls



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

> At least 2 years of professional experience and proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite, specifically Premiere Pro and After Effects

> Bonus if you have experience or proficiency with using 3D software like Blender, Cinema4D, or anything similar.

> You must have strong creative skills and a portfolio of work that demonstrates your passion and experience with video editing and motion graphics

>> Your portfolio must include examples of the type of work listed in the job description section

> Expertise in video editing fundamentals and techniques, including pacing, storytelling, and continuity

> Knowledge of different video formats and codecs for various delivery platforms



PREFERRED EXPERIENCE

> 5 years of experience with Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects.

> At least 2 years of experience and proficiency with Blender or Cinema 4D.

> Proficiency in color correction, audio editing, and mastering

> Familiarity with team communication and project management tools like Smartsheet, Slack, ClickUp, or similar platforms

> Familiarity or experience using AI post-production tools

> Strong understanding of visual composition, typography, and graphic design principles

> Completion of training and/or certifications in key software used

> Knowledge of social media platforms and video encoding specifications for each platform



COMPENSATION & ENGAGEMENT

> Compensation rate for full-time, independent contractor will be $1000 - $1300 USD per month

> Ten paid holidays and ten days of accrued paid time off (PTO) will be included annually

> Rate will be based on qualifications and experience

> This is a remote work position

> Work hours will need to overlap with standard business hours in California (9:00-6:00pm PT)



HOW TO APPLY

If you are interested in working with us as an independent contractor and you meet the required qualifications listed above, please APPLY HERE -

title: Chief Video Editor
description:
The Role

We're a property investment and business education content company producing high volumes across multiple formats. We'll film everything and you'll edit everything, with same-day or next-day turnaround.



Your job is not just cutting footage together. It is storytelling through editing. We send you raw footage that contains the message, and your job is to make that message land as clearly, concisely, and compellingly as possible. That means stripping out every um, every repeated phrase, every 
nt of stumbling, and shaping what remains into something that hooks a viewer in the first second and holds them until the last. Think Alex Hormozi, Iman Gadzhi, Samuel Leeds. Professional, premium, engaging, never brain-rot. The edit should enhance the message, not distract from it.



If you understand the psychology of why viewers scroll, why they stay, and how pacing, cuts, and visual cues keep attention, this role is for you.



What You'll Edit

- Podcast episodes (full-length and clips with jump cuts, captions, B-roll, dynamic zooms)

- Short-form (Reels, TikTok, Shorts with strong hooks, captions, transitions)

-
 ad creatives (direct response with hooks, pattern interrupts, CTAs)

- YouTube long-form (10-30 min educational videos with graphics, lower thirds, retention editing)

- Cinematic real estate videos (drone footage, walk-throughs, colour grading, music sync)

- Everything else the production schedule demands



What You Need



- Expert in Premiere Pro and/or DaVinci Resolve, good knowledge of After Effects

- Ability to take raw, unpolished footage and cut it into a tight, compelling narrative with zero filler. If you have used tools like Descript for AI-assisted cleanup (removing dead air, repeated words, filler sounds), that is a plus, but the skill matters more than the tool.

- Experience editing property/real estate video content including drone footage

- Strong understanding of Hormozi/Gadzhi/Samuel Leeds style editing, pacing for retention, and what makes viewers stay vs scroll

- Reliable 4K-capable machine and fast internet

- AI editing tool experience (Higgsfield, Runway, CapCut AI, Descript) is a strong plus



How to Apply



Start your application with the word REEL or it will not be read. Then include:



Link to your portfolio or showreel (no link = no review)

Which business/property creators you personally watch

Any AI editing tools you've used

Your setup (software, hardware, internet speed) and availability to start



We review your portfolio first. If we like it, we send a paid test edit. If that's strong, we start working together and begin strategizing content angles together. Long-term role with growing volume as we scale. We want one exceptional editor, not five average ones.

title: Bid Coordinator (Construction) – Remote
description:
Bid Coordinator (Construction) – Remote

Company: DK Construction Services LLC

About DK Construction Services LLC

DK Construction Services LLC is a growing construction company based in the United States that specializes in government, municipal, and commercial construction projects. Our company regularly bids on projects from school districts, transportation agencies, municipalities, and other public organizations.

We are looking for a detail-oriented Bid Coordinator who can help manage our bidding process, organize documents, communicate with subcontractors, and ensure bids are prepared accurately and submitted on time.

This role follows a structured Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), so organization, communication, and discipline are essential.



Position Overview

The Bid Coordinator will manage the administrative and coordination aspects of the bidding process. This includes reviewing bid documents, creating bid requirement checklists, coordinating subcontractor outreach, tracking addenda, scheduling site visits, and preparing bid documents ahead of deadlines.

The goal of this role is to ensure DK Construction submits complete, compliant, and competitive bids on time.

Bid Document Review

Subcontractor Coordination

Phone call days

Microsoft Excel

 communication

title: Connecteam Expert Needed to Build Operations System for Residential Care Homes
description:
**Title:**

Connecteam Expert Needed to Build Operations System for Residential Care Homes



**Project Overview**

We operate small residential senior care homes and are implementing Connecteam to manage caregiver workflows, resident care tasks, staff training, onboarding documentation, facility checklists, scheduling, communication, and reporting. We are looking for an experienced freelancer to design and configure a structured Connecteam system so caregivers always know what tasks must be completed during their shifts while management can easily monitor operations.



**Objective**

Build a simple but well-structured Connecteam system that supports caregiver task management, staff onboarding and training, resident care documentation, facility maintenance checklists, scheduling, communication, and dashboards for operational oversight. The system will initially support a 6-resident care home but should be scalable for additional homes.



**Scope of Work**

Configure Connecteam with the following structure:



**Task Categories**

Resident Care, Medication Tracking, Meals & Hydration, Activities & Engagement, Facility Maintenance, Compliance & Safety.



**Resident Care Tasks**

Recurring tasks such as hygiene assistance, diaper/brief checks (some every 2 hours), shower schedules, exercise assistance, mobility support, skin monitoring, bowel movement monitoring, and condition observations.



**Medication Tracking Forms**

Forms for caregivers to log morning medication pass, evening medication pass, PRN medication administration, and medication refusals or issues. Official MAR records will remain in binders.



**Meals & Hydration**

Forms to log meals served, hydration reminders, appetite notes, and optional meal photos.



**Activities Tracking**

Logging of exercises, group activities, walks, music or social engagement, and activity photos/testimonials.



**Facility Maintenance**

Recurring tasks such as cleaning, trash removal, laundry, bathroom sanitation, refrigerator temperature checks, supply restocking, medication cart security, and safety checks. Also create a shared grocery and supply shopping list.



**Compliance & Safety**

Checklists for emergency exits, safety equipment, night safety checks, and facility safety procedures.



**Resident Profiles**

Create a resident face sheet/profile including basic information, contacts, physician/pharmacy info, allergies, and care notes.



**Staff Training System**

Organize training materials including RCFE caregiver training, safety procedures, medication assistance procedures, emergency response guidelines, and ongoing training modules.



**Employee Onboarding**

Set up onboarding documents such as employee handbook, policy acknowledgments, and other documents requiring review and signature.



**Communication**

Configure group chats for each home, announcements, and messaging between caregivers and management.



**Scheduling**

Configure shift scheduling so caregivers can view assigned shifts and management can manage schedules.



**Events Calendar**

Create a calendar for resident birthdays, caregiver birthdays, special events, and activities.



**Dashboards & Reporting**

Create dashboards showing completed tasks, overdue tasks, caregiver activity logs, and i
 reports.



**Documentation**

Provide simple instructions explaining how to add residents, update tasks, manage schedules, and maintain the system.



**Deliverables**

Fully configured Connecteam workspace, task categories and recurring tasks, caregiver forms and checklists, staff training modules, onboarding documentation setup, dashboards/reporting, and simple maintenance documentation.



**Project Details**

Estimated time: 10–15 hours



**Preferred Experience**

Experience with Connecteam or workflow systems such as Airtable, Asana, Monday, Notion, or other task management platforms. Experience designing operational workflows is preferred.



**How to Apply**

Please include a short description of your experience building workflow systems, examples of similar systems you have built, and your estimated time to complete this project.



**Important:**

To confirm that you read the full job description, please begin your proposal with the word **Workflow**. Proposals that do not include this word may not be considered.

title: Executive Assistant + Operations & Visibility Coordinator
description:
Conscious Ketamine Coaching Academy 



Hours

40 hrs/week



Pay

$1,250 USD/month



Start

Immediately



Time Zone

MST overlap 6h+



This is not a beginner VA role. Read carefully before applying.



We are a fast-growing thought leadership and coaching certification company. Our founder — Bronwyn Davis — is a former TV news anchor, 25-year coaching veteran, keynote speaker, and TEDx presenter. She runs the Conscious Ketamine Coaching Academy, training medical professionals and coaches in a breakthrough methodology that transforms client results.



We need one exceptional person who can own three critical areas: Executive Operations, Business Systems, and Visibility/Content. You will be the right hand that keeps everything moving while Bronwyn focuses on coaching, speaking, and leading.



If you need to be told what to do every day — this is not the role for you. If you are a self-directed operator who builds systems, thinks ahead, and takes pride in making a business run clean — keep reading.



What you will own



1. Executive inbox & CEO operations

Own Bronwyn's inbox daily — keep it at zero

Draft professional responses in her voice

Merge and manage two
 accounts (Gmail + Outlook) into one clean workflow

Extract action items from Fathom AI recordings after every call

Build and maintain a weekly CEO Dashboard in Asana

Track proposals, invoices, and all follow-ups — zero revenue leaks

Follow up on unpaid invoices professionally and persistently



2. Client experience

Bi-weekly check-ins with all active clients

Track coaching deliverables, milestone progress, and deadlines

Prepare call recap summaries

Ensure every client feels premium-level support



3. Operations & systems (you will build this from scratch)

Build and manage all project systems in Asana

Set up Zapier connections between Asana and GoHighLevel

Create SOPs as systems develop

Deliver a weekly CEO KPI scoreboard: new leads, strategy calls booked/held, conversions, speaking pitches sent/booked, newsletter sends (1x/week — non-negotiable)



4. Visibility, content & podcasting updated

Take Fathom/Zoom recordings into Descript for editing and repurposing

Create short-form social clips from long-form video

Draft and schedule weekly newsletters in Flodesk

Manage 3–5 social media posts per week repurposed from newsletter/podcast/speaking content

Podcast production end-to-end — edit episodes in Descript, write show notes, upload and publish via BuzzSprout, distribute to Spotify, Apple, and YouTube

Repurpose podcast episodes into social clips, quote graphics, and newsletter content

Research and pitch aligned podcasts for Bronwyn to guest on — outreach, follow-up, and booking

Maintain a podcast guest pipeline and track all outreach and bookings

Coordinate with podcast guests — scheduling, prep materials, recording logistics

Research aligned speaking opportunities and customize pitch templates

Track all outreach metrics and follow-ups



5. GoHighLevel CRM management

Maintain CRM, pipelines, and lead tags

Deploy and monitor automated follow-up sequences (6–8 touches)

Build retreat interest sequences and nurture flows

Connect GoHighLevel with Asana via Zapier

Deliver weekly sales pipeline report

Tools you must know (or learn fast)



Required from day one

GoHighLevel (GHL) — CRM and automations

Asana — project management (you will build the boards)

Fathom — AI meeting notes (you will mine recordings for tasks)

Google Workspace — Gmail, Calendar, Drive

Descript — podcast editing and content repurposing

BuzzSprout — podcast hosting and distribution

Zoom — recording and collaboration



Nice to have

Zapier (platform automations)

CapCut (mobile video editing)

AI tools — ChatGPT, Claude, etc. (comfort required)



You are the right person if...

You have 3+ years supporting coaches, consultants, or speakers at an executive level

You have built systems from scratch — not just followed them

You have managed or produced a podcast before — end to end

Your written and spoken English is excellent — non-negotiable

You work independently without needing daily direction

You communicate proactively before things become problems

You are comfortable following up on unpaid invoices and proposals

You have managed social media or podcast content for a personal brand before

You can overlap at least 6 hours with Arizona (MST) daily



How to apply — read this carefully

Do not send a generic message. We will not read it. To be considered, you must answer all five questions below in your application message. Short or vague answers will disqualify you immediately. 



Start your message with the word CONSCIOUS so we know you read this in full.

Question 1

Walk me through exactly how you would manage an executive's inbox for 3 days with no direction from the founder. Be specific.

Question 2

If you were asked to book 3 podcast interviews per month for a thought leadership client, what would your step-by-step process look like — from research to booked?

Question 3

Describe a time you identified a broken process or system and fixed it without being asked. What was the problem, what did you do, and what was the result?

Question 4

How do you prioritize when everything feels urgent and the CEO is unavailable? Give a real example.

Question 5

Are you comfortable following up on unpaid invoices on behalf of a founder? Describe exactly how you would handle a client who has not responded after two reminders.



We read every answer. This is how you get to the next round.

title: SEO Expert Wanted (UK Hours)
description:

title: TikTok Shop Video Editor (12 Videos Per Day, Same-Day Delivery)
description:
The ideal candidate has proven experience editing short-form vertical videos for TikTok or TikTok Shop and understands how to cut raw footage into fast, engaging videos optimized for pacing, flow, and watch time. This role requires efficiency and speed, as the editor will be responsible for producing 12 videos per day with same-day delivery.



Strong English listening comprehension is required. Much of the raw footage includes multiple takes of the same line, and the editor must be able to clearly understand spoken English in order to confidently choose the best take based on delivery, clarity, and tone without needing step-by-step guidance.



The editor must be comfortable working with UGC-style raw footage and following provided references exactly. This includes using jump cuts, zooms, basic motion, sound effects when appropriate, and adding product images or assets that are provided. No captions or subtitles are required for these edits.



Speed, reliability, and consistency are critical. The current workload of 12 videos typically takes approximately 2–3 hours per day, so the editor must be able to work efficiently while maintaining quality and meeting same-day deadlines.



Technical proficiency with CapCut, Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar video editing software is required. The editor should also be comfortable exporting videos in TikTok-ready vertical formats and performing basic audio cleanup and leveling when needed.



This role requires someone who can work independently, make good judgment calls, accept feedback, and implement revisions quickly. This is not a beginner position and is intended as a long-term, full-time role for the right candidate.



If you apply please include 2 or 3 examples of your previous work.

title: Part-time CapCut / TikTok Video Editor (TikTok Shop) — Potential for More
description:
Hiring for part-time work for a CapCut / TikTok editor.



You will be making TikTok videos using CapCut or the video editing software of your choice. The editing process is fairly simple and straightforward. Anyone who is proficient in video editing should have no problem with the basic tasks such as:



* Cutting dead space in talking head videos

* Adding overlay images on screen in videos

* Minor effects such as dynamic zoom, cropping, etc.

* Producing b-roll content from voiceover and footage provided, with the ability to understand what is being said and pair the audio with corresponding footage



Please review a few examples of my content to make sure you are familiar and comfortable with the editing style. A strong understanding of English and social media content is crucial here.



Basic talking head

Here you are mostly editing dead space and piecing clips together, adding minor effects like crop and zoom to make the frames look more dynamic and seamless.





Talking head with overlays

Same as before, but adding images and green screen backgrounds provided to you to support what is being said in the video.





B-roll content

You will be provided b-roll clips along with a reference video and will need to work through the footage to find fitting visuals.





You will be working hand in hand with me on an extremely viral social media page, and there is room for bonuses if deadlines are met and the content performs well.



Requirements:



* Attention to detail

* Strong English

* Strong internet connection

* Previous experience with CapCut / TikTok / Instagram

* Works well with a team

* Ability to learn and take constructive feedback

* Availability to work 2–3 hours a day, with potential for more and increased salary

* Able to work 5–7 days per week

* Can take payments through Wise or PayPal

* PH or similar time zone

$400/month + bonuses for 2–3 hours a day max, with potential full-time work in the future and a salary increase.



To apply, please complete the recreate assignment below so I can see your skills (1 hour max).

- Flexible schedule: Choose your own hours, as long as you show up for the daily meeting (MANDATORY)

- Stable income: Long-term role with regular, on-time payments

- Supportive team: Collaborative environment without micromanagement

- Growth path: Opportunities to increase responsibilities and earnings

- 100% remote: Work-from-home setup



TASKS

- Run kickoff calls with new clients using our proven script

- Review client content and give actionable feedback

- Answer client messages within 24 hours

- Host weekly group calls and 1-on-1 coaching sessions

- Help clients overcome content blocks and limiting beliefs



Progress Tracking:



- Update daily/weekly reports

- Track client progress and next steps

- Join daily team check-ins

- Ensure clients follow strategies consistently



REQUIREMENTS (MUST-HAVE)



- C1+ English (written and spoken) with no accent

- Strong communication skills

- Organized and detail-oriented

- Reliable internet and a quiet workspace

- Accountability mindset and deadline commitment



IMPORTANT



When recording a Loom video as part of your application:



- Camera must show your FACE & SCREEN

- Treat it as a LIVE interview with the Hiring Manager

- Have a professional background

- Have clear audio

- Wear business-casual attire



BONUS SKILLS (NOT REQUIRED)



- Content creation and social media experience

- Knowledge of Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts

- Regular usage of AI tools (ChatGPT, CapCut)



WHY JOIN US?



- Start rate: $935 a month + performance-based bonuses (TOTAL: $1,100 a month)

Note: More experienced CSMs earn between $1,800 and $2,500 after 6-12 months.

- Performance bonuses: Up to $900+ additional monthly based on:

- Client retention rates

- Task completion metrics

- Quality of feedback

- Team collaboration

- Payments: Twice per month via Wise directly to your bank

- Tools access: We'll cover all the needed tools

- Work-life balance: No graveyard shifts

- Professional growth: Regular feedback and skill development



HOW TO APPLY

#5: Your portfolio.



Note: Messages that do not follow this format will NOT be reviewed.



P.S. Young Publisher is the recruitment company. Successful candidates will work under Social Influence Consulting LLC.

title: Operations Lead / Chief of Staff
description:
We're a US-based insurtech startup with a small engineering team based in Bohol. The founder is an engineer — great at building product, not so great at staying organized. We need someone who keeps the whole operation running smoothly so the team can focus on shipping.



This is not a virtual assistant role. You'll be the operational backbone of the company — sitting with the team daily, running the day-to-day, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks. If you're the kind of person who gets genuinely annoyed when things are disorganized, this is your dream job.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN:

Daily standups and team coordination — making sure the "one big task" for the day is clear and everyone is unblocked

Hiring pipeline — screening resumes, scheduling interviews, coordinating trial weeks for candidates

Contractor admin — onboarding paperwork, payment tracking via Wise, ensuring everyone gets paid on time

Vendor and supplier coordination — office setup, internet, equipment procurement

Calendar and communications — managing the founder's schedule, filtering inbound, drafting responses

Light project management — tracking features, bugs, and deadlines in Linear/Notion

Local operations — coordinating with our registered local entity for BIR filings, permits, and compliance

Budget tracking — monitoring monthly burn, flagging cost issues early



REQUIREMENTS:

You are based in Bohol or willing to relocate — this is a fully on-site role in Panglao

Extremely organized and detail-oriented — you live in spreadsheets, checklists, and systems

Strong English communication — written and verbal, as you'll interact with US-based contacts

Experience managing operations, admin, or project coordination (startup experience is a huge plus)

Comfortable with tools like Notion, Slack, Google Workspace, and project management software

You take ownership — you don't wait to be told what to do, you see the gap and fill it

You can context-switch between "schedule a meeting" and "negotiate an office lease" in the same hour



NICE TO HAVE:

Experience in tech startups or working with engineering teams

Familiarity with Philippine business registration (DTI, BIR, barangay permits)

Bookkeeping or basic accounting knowledge

Experience hiring and managing contractors or freelancers



WHAT WE OFFER:

Competitive PHP salary paid twice monthly

On-site role in Panglao, Bohol — work directly with the founder and engineering team

Meal and transport allowance

You'll be employee #3 at a funded startup — massive growth potential

Real responsibility from day one, not busy work



Start your application with the word "lighthouse" so we know you read this.

title: Marketplace Management
description:
We are looking for a data-driven Marketplace Growth Specialist to take ownership of our presence on platforms  . This is not a customer service or order processing role. Your mission is to protect our "Digital Shelf," analyze performance trends, and proactively drive revenue growth through strategic optimization.



Key Responsibilities



Performance Analytics: Conduct weekly and monthly deep dives into Year-over-Year (YOY) and Month-over-Month (MOM) sales data to identify trends, wins, and red flags.



Revenue Investigation: Actively investigate why key "Hero Products" may be seeing a decline in sales and implement recovery strategies (e.g., pricing adjustments, content refreshes, competition investigation)



Digital Shelf Management: Ensure our products are winning the "digital shelf." This includes monitoring search rankings, ensuring high-quality imagery/SEO, and checking that we are categorized correctly versus competitors.



Channel Strategy: Identify opportunities for promotion, better visibility, and portfolio expansion within the platform’s ecosystem.



Reporting: Present clear, actionable insights to management rather than just raw data.



Requirements



Analytical Mindset: You must be comfortable with Excel/Google Sheets and enjoy "connecting the dots" between data points and real-world sales.



Marketplace Experience: Previous experience managing UK-based marketplaces (The Range, Amazon, eBay, etc.) is highly preferred.



Problem Solver: You don’t just report that sales are down; you come to the table with three reasons why and a plan to fix it.



Attention to Detail: High standards for how our brand is represented on the platform.

title: AI User & Capcut Video Content Editor
description:
Hi there!



I’m looking for a motivated and reliable Virtual Assistant to help create AI recipe content using modern AI tools and simple editing workflows.



This role is ideal for someone who is curious about AI, excited by content creation, and willing to learn step by step. You do not need to be an expert already, but you do need to be genuinely interested in the space and open to improving.



You’ll be working with tools such as Veo Flow, HeyGen, ElevenLabs, and CapCut to help turn recipe ideas into engaging short-form videos.



This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to build valuable skills in AI content creation, short-form video production, and viral-style content workflows.



What You’ll Be Helping With



AI Content Creation



Following a step-by-step process to create AI recipe videos



Using AI tools to help generate scenes, voiceovers, visuals, and content ideas



Learning how different AI tools work together in a content workflow



Organizing prompts, assets, and project files

Veo Flow



HeyGen



ElevenLabs



CapCut



Video Editing -



Editing short-form videos for platforms like TikTok, Reels, and Shorts



Adding captions, transitions, sound, music, and simple effects



Cleaning up clips and making videos feel smooth and engaging



General Support



Following instructions carefully



Keeping projects organized



Communicating progress clearly



Contributing ideas when appropriate



Helping improve the content process over time



Who This Role Is For



I’m mainly looking for someone who is:



Very willing to learn



Curious about AI and content creation



Passionate about online content and how videos perform



Reliable and consistent



Comfortable following a structured process



Open to a little creativity within the system



Interested in learning how to make content that has viral potential



You do not need years of experience. What matters more is your attitude, effort, curiosity, and willingness to improve.



Skills I’m Looking For -



Important



Basic familiarity with CapCut



Good English communication



Attention to detail



Ability to follow instructions step by step



Organized and dependable



Helpful but not required



Any exposure to AI tools



Interest in short-form content



Basic understanding of TikTok, Reels, or Shorts



Creative eye for pacing and engaging edits



Pay and Growth



Starting pay: $3/hour



Progression available for the right person



Long-term opportunity if it is a good fit



You’ll also gain valuable hands-on skills in:



AI tools



content creation



video editing



short-form content workflows



viral-style content production



Why This Role Is Valuable



This is not just a basic VA job. You’ll be learning practical skills in one of the fastest-growing areas online right now: AI-driven content creation.



If you are someone who wants to grow, build experience, and become highly skilled in AI content and editing, this could be a very good opportunity.



How to Apply

Your experience with CapCut or any video editing

Your availability and preferred working hours

Any sample edits



Any content you’ve worked on before



Any AI tools you’ve tried or want to learn



I’m looking for someone who is hungry to learn, easy to work with, and excited to grow in this space.



If that sounds like you, I’d love to hear from you.

title: Video Producer – Short Form Content
description:
About 37 Projects

We are the #1 Video Podcast Marketing Service for Health & Performance Brands.

We help top experts, nutritionists, doctors, and wellness professionals grow their YouTube presence with engaging, high-quality content.



About the Role

37 Projects produces large volumes of YouTube Shorts, clips, and podcast content for leading voices in health, wellness, and performance.

We already have video editors, content managers, and quality checkers.

However, we are looking for someone who can protect the creative quality of the content before it is delivered to the client.

This role sits between the editors and final approval.

Your job is to ensure the videos are visually strong, engaging, and aligned with brand style before they are finalized.



What You Will Do

Review completed videos and evaluate:

• Hook strength in the first seconds

• Visual emphasis of key 
nts

• Animation quality and motion graphics

• Caption readability and pacing

• Visual storytelling clarity

• Overall engagement of the Short



If a video is weak or incomplete, you provide clear feedback to the editor for improvement.

Example feedback:

Instead of: “Animation is weak.”

You might say: “This sentence should have a full-screen text emphasis instead of a small caption highlight.”



Your Responsibilities

• Review Shorts, clips, and trailers before final approval

• Ensure animations and captions follow brand guidelines

• Improve visual storytelling when needed

• Provide clear feedback to editors in 
 or similar tools

• Help maintain consistent creative standards across clients



You Are a Good Fit If You

• Have strong instincts for short-form storytelling

• Can immediately see when a video feels weak or slow

• Understand pacing, emphasis, and viewer attention

• Have experience reviewing or directing video edits

• Are comfortable giving feedback to editors



Important

This role is not primarily editing videos.



Your main responsibility is creative review and feedback.



Ideal Background

• Video editor with strong storytelling instincts

• YouTube producer

• Short-form content specialist

• Motion graphics editor with creative direction experience



Tools We Use

• Adobe Premiere Pro

• After Effects

• 

• Notion

• Slack





Typical workload:

• Reviewing multiple Shorts and clips per day

• Providing feedback to editors



How to Apply

title: Trailer Architect / Podcast Story Producer Application
description:
About 37 Projects



37 Projects is a YouTube-first video podcast marketing agency working with health, wellness, and performance brands. We help experts, doctors, and founders turn long-form conversations into high-retention YouTube videos that educate, build trust, and grow audiences.



We are not a generic content agency. Our work is research-driven, audience-aware, and optimized for YouTube discovery and retention.



About the Role

37 Projects produces podcast content for health experts, doctors, researchers, and performance brands. From every podcast episode we create multiple assets: full episodes, clips, Shorts, and high-impact trailers.



The trailer is one of the most important pieces of content we produce. It needs to capture the emotional tension, ideas, and story of the episode in a short, powerful format.



We are looking for someone who can design the architecture of these trailers.

This role is not about editing. It is about story judgment.

You will watch the full podcast episode and determine which 
nts belong in the trailer and in what order to create the strongest narrative.

Video editors will then execute the edit based on your blueprint.



What You Will Do

• Watch full podcast episodes (1–2 hours)

• Identify the most powerful, emotional, or thought-provoking 
nts

• Select 15–20 candidate clips for the trailer

• Build the story structure of the trailer

• Arrange the clips in a way that creates curiosity, tension, and payoff

• Deliver a clear timestamp blueprint for editors



Example deliverable:



TRAILER STRUCTURE

0:00 Hook

12:14 – Key provocative idea

0:07 Curiosity 
nt

05:58 – Unexpected question

0:16 Emotional insight

20:56 – Personal reflection

0:28 Thought-provoking idea

17:33 – Key concept

0:38 Closing line



Editors then produce the final trailer using this structure.



You Are a Good Fit If You

• Have a strong editorial eye for storytelling

• Understand pacing, emotional beats, and narrative tension

• Can identify 
nts that make viewers curious

• Are comfortable watching long-form conversations and extracting the best parts

• Have experience with YouTube, documentaries, podcast editing, journalism, or narrative storytelling



This Role Is NOT

? Video editing

? Motion graphics design

? Thumbnail creation

This role is about content judgment and storytelling architecture.



Nice to Have

• Experience editing podcast content

• Experience producing YouTube content

• Experience with documentary storytelling

• Familiarity with YouTube audience psychology



Work Setup

Remote work with flexible hours.



Typical workload:

• 16-24 podcast episodes per month

• Each episode requires 1 trailer blueprint



How to Apply

title: YouTube Shorts Video Editor (with After Effects)
description:
About 37 Projects

We are the #1 Video Podcast Marketing Service for Health & Performance Brands.

We help top experts, nutritionists, doctors, and wellness professionals grow their YouTube presence with engaging, high-quality content.

Now we’re hiring a YouTube Shorts Video Editor with advanced motion graphics skills to join our growing team.





Role Overview

We’re looking for an editor who specializes in fast-paced, high-retention YouTube Shorts and is highly skilled in After Effects motion graphics.

Your work must go beyond simple cutting—you must be able to enhance storytelling through kinetic typography, animated elements, transitions, and visually dynamic scenes.



You’ll be given raw footage and reference edits. Your job: replicate the style, elevate it, and deliver polished 9:16 vertical videos.



Key Responsibilities

- Edit 60–90 second YouTube Shorts using high-retention editing techniques.

- Create custom motion graphics in After Effects (this is a MUST).

- Design high-quality kinetic text, animated highlights, and visual callouts.

- Use smooth transitions, multilayer motion graphics, and dynamic visual pacing.

- Add sound design: music, subtle SFX, and emphasis pops.

- Apply accurate, engaging captions styled for high retention.

- Follow the editing style of our provided samples (Types C, D, E).

- Deliver export-ready 9:16 vertical videos optimized for YouTube Shorts.



Who You Are

- You’ve edited high-retention short-form content (YouTube Shorts, Reels, TikTok).

- You have advanced After Effects motion graphics skills—non-negotiable.

- You’re skilled in Premiere Pro (or DaVinci Resolve) and integrate AE seamlessly.

- You understand dynamic pacing and storytelling for vertical video.

- You can create clean, modern motion design that enhances clarity and emotion.

- Strong sense of design: timing, spacing, typography, colors, visual rhythm.

- Excellent sound design skills: enhancing beats, transitions, and emotional peaks.

- Attention to detail: no choppy cuts, no awkward pauses, no sloppy captions.

- Fast, reliable, and able to deliver consistent high-quality results.



Our 4 Core Principles at 37 Projects

Competence – Always improve, deliver your best, and think strategically.

Commitment – Meet deadlines, own your work, and deliver on time.

mber.

title: Assistant Property Manager / Operations - Buildium
description:
Assistant Property Manager (Buildium) – Full Time



Portfolio: ~200 Residential Units

Position Type: Full-Time (40 hours/week)



Overview



We are seeking a highly organized Remote Assistant Property Manager with experience using Buildium to support the day-to-day operations of our growing portfolio of approximately 200 residential units.



This role will work closely with the CEO and our physical property manager to ensure operations run smoothly across all properties.



The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, organized, proactive, and comfortable managing systems across a large portfolio.



Key Responsibilities

Assistant to the Property Manager and Operatuions support 



• Maintain accurate property and tenant records in Buildium

• Assist the CEO and physical property manager with operational tasks

• Maintain organized digital property files and documentation

• Monitor internal workflows to ensure tasks are completed on time



Vacancy & Marketing Oversight



Ensure vacant units are properly marketed and competitive in the market.



Responsibilities include:



• Confirm listings are posted on major rental platforms (Zillow, 
, etc.)

• Review listings for photo quality, pricing accuracy, and strong descriptions

• Monitor listing performance and flag units that may need improvements

• Ensure units remain visible and competitive online



Vacancy Tracking & Leasing Pipeline



Maintain a vacancy tracker and leasing pipeline including:



• vacant units

• days on market

• showings scheduled

• applications received

• approved applicants

• units pending lease signing



This allows leadership to quickly identify problem units or slow-moving listings.



Lease Expiration Monitoring







Operational Reporting



Provide a weekly operations summary including:



• current vacancies

• applications received

• leases signed

• upcoming lease expirations

• general portfolio activity



Bookkeeping Oversight. Must be proficient in Buildium accounting. 



Requirements



• Experience using Buildium 

• Prior experience working with a property management company

• Strong organizational and operational skills

• Ability to manage tasks across a 200-unit portfolio

• Excellent written communication

• Comfortable working remotely and independently



Preferred Qualifications



• Experience supporting 100+ unit portfolios

• Experience with vacancy marketing and rental listings

• Experience supporting property management operations



Hours



Full-time (50 hours per week)

Remote position supporting a portfolio of ~200 residential units.



Long-term opportunity with a growing property management company.



***IMPORTANT*** This role requires your full focus during scheduled working hours. Working other jobs while on the clock, misrepresenting hours, or attempting to bypass time tracking is strictly prohibited and will result in immediate termination. We utilize Time Doctor to monitor work hours and productivity including screenshots, video recordings and tracking of mouse movements to discourage and prevent theft of time. 



Application Instructions



Please answer the following questions in your application:



1. Are you currently working any other full-time or part-time jobs? If yes, please explain.



2. Can you commit to working exclusively for us during the scheduled hours for this role?



3. Have you ever performed any tasks related to overall operations?
